hypothesis: If FREED++ optimizes a dual objective that maximizes pharmacophoric similarity to a reference set of estrogen receptor antagonists/degraders while minimizing structural similarity to known compounds, then the generated molecules will show high pharmacophore similarity to ER ligands and high novelty (low similarity to public databases) while maintaining docking scores and QED values comparable to or better than baselines, because the dual objectives steer generation toward ER-relevant pharmacophore patterns and novelty without sacrificing drug-likeness.
